confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_280e20f8-d7f1-4781-8891-3fb18268d29d read back as received

containment: Stop using OSM delivery for affected identities until binding uniqueness is verified.
Inspect runtime binding file and live HWNDs before further repair.
Restart overlay only after binding state is normalized.
corrective_action: Add binding reconciliation command that atomically normalizes runtime bindings for a Site/PC: remove dead HWNDs, reject duplicate HWND identity bindings, reject duplicate live singleton identity bindings, and write one canonical binding set.
Make identity binding mutation transactional: read current state, validate uniqueness intent, apply update, validate postcondition, then write.
Make overlay start/reload destroy all stale label windows owned by the overlay process before rendering current labels.
Make overlay renderer enforce one rendered label per live HWND even if input contains duplicates; duplicates should surface as diagnostics, not duplicate UI.
Make OSM delivery preflight refuse ambiguous or duplicate bindings before focusing/sending.

symptoms: Two labels appeared on each affected Windows Terminal window.
Labels visually overlaid and mixed, making identity unreadable.
Runtime bindings showed Kevin and Bob swapped across HWNDs during repair attempts.
OSM subsequently resolved Bob to Kevin surface because binding truth was polluted.

preventive_action: Add regression tests for duplicate identity, duplicate HWND, dead HWND cleanup, overlay restart idempotence, and OSM refusal on ambiguous binding.
Add a compact operator-surface health command that reports binding uniqueness, overlay label count per HWND, stale HWNDs, and OSM delivery readiness.
Document runtime binding as a projection that must be reconciled from explicit evidence, not edited ad hoc.
root_cause_hypothesis: Operator-surface binding mutation is non-transactional: it can leave stale duplicate identity/HWND associations after repair.

Runtime binding authority does not enforce uniqueness constraints: one HWND must not resolve to multiple identities, and one singleton OSA identity must not resolve to multiple live HWNDs unless explicitly modeled.
Overlay renderer/restart path is not idempotent: stale label windows can survive or duplicate instead of being destroyed before re-rendering.
OSM delivery trusts polluted runtime bindings without sanity-checking uniqueness before delivery.
payload.summary: After an operator-surface label repair attempt, the runtime binding state became contradictory across Kevin and Bob surfaces and the native overlay displayed duplicate overlapping labels on each window. This shows a second failure mode beyond ambient foreground binding: runtime binding reconciliation and overlay rendering are not idempotent or uniqueness-enforced.
payload.title: CAPA: enforce one-HWND-one-label and transactional operator-surface binding reconciliation
verification: Given duplicate bindings for one HWND, reconcile refuses or normalizes to one binding with recorded evidence.
Given one singleton identity bound to two live HWNDs, reconcile refuses unless a declared multi-surface policy exists.
Starting overlay twice results in one overlay label per bound live HWND.
OSM to Bob cannot resolve to Kevin when Bob and Kevin bindings are contradictory; it must fail with binding_ambiguous.
